definition: to combine ideas or information into a clearer whole
example: In our essay we synthesise evidence from two sources.

definition: to judge the value or quality of something
example: Evaluate how effective the author's argument is.

definition: to work out an idea from clues in the text
example: We can infer that the character feels guilty.

definition: to explain the meaning of something
example: Interpret the graph before writing your answer.

definition: to add notes to a text while reading
example: Annotate the article before the class discussion.

definition: to restate an idea using different words
example: Paraphrase the sentence instead of copying it.

definition: to add more detail to an idea
example: Elaborate on your point with a clear example.

definition: to make someone believe or do something
example: The speech tries to persuade students to volunteer.

definition: a story or account of events
example: The narrative is written from a child's point of view.

definition: the main argument in an essay
example: A strong thesis makes the essay easier to follow.

definition: connected clearly and smoothly
example: Use linking words to make your paragraph cohesive.

definition: using few words but still clear
example: Keep your explanation concise and accurate.

definition: connected to the topic or question
example: Choose evidence that is relevant to your argument.

definition: to repeat exact words from a text
example: Quote one sentence from the novel to support your point.

definition: a note that shows where information came from
example: Add a citation after the borrowed idea.

definition: a list of sources used in a piece of work
example: Put the bibliography at the end of your report.

definition: the first part of a piece of writing
example: The introduction explains the main topic.

definition: a group of sentences about one main idea
example: Start a new paragraph when the idea changes.

definition: the words used in a language or subject
example: Build your vocabulary by reading every day.

definition: the situation around an idea or event
example: Use context to understand the meaning of the word.

definition: a main idea in a text
example: Friendship is an important theme in the story.

definition: the way a writer creates a character
example: The characterisation shows that the hero is insecure.

definition: a serious disagreement or problem
example: The main conflict begins when the family moves town.

definition: the part where a problem is solved
example: The resolution explains what happens after the battle.

definition: a comparison that says one thing is another
example: The writer uses a metaphor to describe fear.

definition: a comparison using like or as
example: The simile helps the reader imagine the storm.

definition: language that creates pictures in the mind
example: The imagery makes the scene feel peaceful.

definition: the use of objects to represent ideas
example: The red scarf is an example of symbolism.

definition: a method used to achieve an effect
example: Repetition is a technique used in speeches.

definition: the people who read or watch something
example: Think about the audience before choosing your tone.

definition: a test done to learn or prove something
example: We repeated the experiment to check the result.

definition: to study something carefully to find facts
example: The class will investigate how plants grow.

definition: to say what may happen in the future
example: Predict what will happen when the liquid is heated.

definition: to put things into groups
example: Classify the animals by their features.

definition: to watch carefully and notice details
example: Observe the colour change during the reaction.

definition: to find the size or amount of something
example: Measure the temperature every two minutes.

definition: to write down information
example: Record your results in the table.

definition: a simple drawing that explains something
example: Label the diagram of the human heart.

definition: a very small piece of matter
example: Particles move faster when a substance is heated.

definition: the smallest unit of a chemical element
example: An atom has protons and electrons.

definition: a group of atoms joined together
example: A water molecule contains hydrogen and oxygen.

definition: the basic unit of living things
example: Plant cells have a cell wall.

definition: a living thing
example: A mushroom is a living organism.

definition: a community of living things and their environment
example: The pond ecosystem includes plants and insects.

definition: the ability to do work or cause change
example: Food gives the body energy.

definition: a push or pull that can change motion
example: Friction is a force that slows objects down.

definition: movement from one place or position to another
example: The graph shows the motion of the bike.

definition: a change that happens when substances interact
example: The reaction produced bubbles.

definition: a mixture where one substance dissolves in another
example: Salt water is a solution.

definition: a number that represents part of a whole
example: One half is a common fraction.

definition: a number written with a decimal point
example: Convert the fraction to a decimal.

definition: a number shown as part of one hundred
example: Calculate the percentage of students who agreed.

definition: a comparison between two amounts
example: The ratio of boys to girls is three to two.

definition: a part or share of a whole
example: A large proportion of the class chose soccer.

definition: a type of maths using letters and symbols
example: Algebra helps us solve unknown values.

definition: a maths phrase with numbers or variables
example: Simplify the expression before solving it.

definition: a rule written with symbols
example: Use the formula to find the area.

definition: a diagram that shows data or relationships
example: Draw a graph to show the results.

definition: a number that shows position on a grid
example: Plot the coordinate on the graph.

definition: the steepness of a line
example: Find the gradient of the line on the graph.

definition: the amount of space inside a flat shape
example: Calculate the area of the rectangle.

definition: the amount of space inside a 3D object
example: Find the volume of the prism.

definition: the distance around a shape
example: Add the side lengths to find the perimeter.

definition: the chance that something will happen
example: The probability of rolling a six is one sixth.

definition: a society with cities culture and government
example: Ancient Egypt was a powerful civilisation.

definition: movement from one place to another
example: Migration changed the population of the city.

definition: a place where people establish a community
example: The settlement grew near the river.

definition: the group that controls a country or area
example: The government made a new law.

definition: a system where people choose leaders by voting
example: Australia is a democracy.

definition: the system of money jobs and trade in a place
example: Tourism is important to the local economy.

definition: the buying and selling of goods
example: Trade helped the town become wealthy.

definition: something useful that people can use
example: Water is an important natural resource.

definition: the natural world around us
example: Pollution can damage the environment.

definition: the ability to continue without damaging the future
example: Sustainability is important in modern farming.

definition: the qualities that make a person or group who they are
example: Language can be part of a person's identity.

definition: the presence of many different types of people or things
example: Australia has great cultural diversity.

definition: the state of having the same rights or chances
example: Equality means everyone is treated fairly.

definition: basic freedoms or protections that people should have
example: Students have rights and responsibilities at school.

definition: a duty to do something or care for something
example: It is your responsibility to bring your laptop.

definition: a planned meeting or visit
example: I have a dentist appointment after school.

definition: the movement of people or goods
example: Buses are a common form of transport.

definition: approval to do something
example: Ask for permission before leaving class.

definition: a regular way of doing things
example: A study routine can help you stay organised.

definition: a plan for how money will be used
example: Make a budget before buying new supplies.

definition: a piece of paper that shows payment
example: Keep the receipt in case you need a refund.

definition: to arrange things in a clear way
example: Organise your notes before the test.

definition: to discuss in order to reach an agreement
example: The group had to negotiate a fair plan.

definition: to say sorry for something
example: Apologise if you interrupt someone.

definition: to ask for something politely
example: You can request help from the teacher.

definition: worried or nervous about something
example: I felt anxious before the oral presentation.

definition: willing to work hard toward a goal
example: I feel motivated when I understand the task.

definition: not giving up easily
example: She was determined to improve her English.

definition: able to do things without much help
example: Independent learners ask questions and try again.

definition: able to be trusted to do the right thing
example: A responsible student submits work on time.

definition: continuing even when something is hard
example: Be persistent when learning difficult words.

definition: able to change when situations change
example: An adaptable student can learn in a new school.

definition: showing respect for others
example: Use respectful language in class discussions.

definition: to work with others on a task
example: Collaborate with your group to finish the project.

definition: to think carefully about learning or experience
example: Reflect on what you did well after the assessment.

definition: the ability to start things without being told
example: Showing initiative can help you succeed at school.
